text.title: SDD Promotion Gate Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the sdd-promotion-gate-checklist methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Every checklist item MUST be binary (yes/no, file exists, link returns 200).
rule.rationale: Subjective criteria turn the gate into gut-feel and erode trust.

rule.statement: Checklist MUST verify presence of spec.md, design.md, test-plan.md before promotion.
rule.rationale: Missing canonical files prevent task generation.

rule.statement: Checklist MUST name the reviewer signing off; '@team' is forbidden.
Checklist MUST enumerate open blockers; any non-empty list blocks promotion.
rule.rationale: Hidden blockers surface in in-progress and stall sprints.

rule.statement: Each `yes` MUST link to file / PR / decision URL.
Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
